title: Diapedium laetum
intro: Diapedium laetum, also known as the New Zealand woodbine, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of Diapedium laetum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Diapedium laetum is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
